title: Fix sessionManagement middleware test FK failures

title: Subtask 4.2: Fix frontend and root package vulnerabilities (7 remaining)
description: Fix 3 frontend vulnerabilities (react-router-dom, react-router, @remix-run/router - XSS via open redirects CVSS 8.0) and 4-5 root package vulnerabilities (express, body-parser, qs, lodash, tar). Apply npm audit fix in both locations. Time: 1-2 hours.

title: Fix RegisterPage validation test timing issues (14 failing tests)
Subtask 4.2.1: Investigate and resolve remaining 5 GitHub security vulnerabilities
description: GitHub reports 5 vulnerabilities (4 high, 1 moderate) despite npm audit showing 0 across all packages. Investigate discrepancy: check GitHub Advisory Database, analyze transitive dependencies, verify package-lock.json consistency, and resolve any actual vulnerabilities. Time: 1-2 hours.

title: Subtask 4.3: Implement security alerting system
description: Integrate Sentry or similar error tracking system for security event monitoring. Configure alert thresholds for critical security events (auth failures, IDOR attempts, rate limit violations). Set up dashboards for security metrics visualization. Implement email/Slack notifications for high-priority alerts. Time: 6-8 hours.

title: Implement retry buttons for failed data fetches in Progression Tab
description: Add retry button UI to error states in Progression Tab components (XPProgressBar, StatProgressionChart, RecentGains, AgeUpCounter, TrainingRecommendations). Currently when data fetch fails, components show error message but no retry option. Test exists but skipped (HorseDetailPage.ProgressionTab.test.tsx:636 - 'should provide retry button for failed data fetches'). Components should add retry button that calls React Query's refetch() method to retry failed API calls.

title: Align frontend training client to backend UUID and status record
description: Frontend still passes numeric userId to /training/trainable/:userId and assumes array status; backend expects UUID and returns discipline-keyed object. Align identifiers and parsing to prevent request failures and data leaks.

title: Subtask 4.1: Fix npm dependency vulnerabilities (4 vulnerabilities: 3 high, 1 moderate)
description: Fix 4 npm dependency vulnerabilities: qs (DoS via memory exhaustion), lodash (Prototype Pollution), express and body-parser (depend on vulnerable qs). Updates: qs 6.13.0→6.14.1, lodash 4.17.21→4.17.23, express 4.21.2→4.22.1, body-parser 1.20.3→1.20.4. Time: 1-2 hours including testing.

Subtask 3.1: Rate Limiting for Registration/Profile Endpoints
description: Add rate limiting to registration and profile endpoints. Configure appropriate limits: /api/auth/register (5 attempts per 15 min), /api/auth/profile GET/PUT (30 requests per 15 min), /api/users/:id (50 requests per 15 min). Test rate limiting behavior with integration tests. Time: 6-8 hours.

comments.author: HOPE2SB\heirr
comments.text:
Rate limiter implementation complete (~70% of subtask done):

✅ Completed:
- Added profileRateLimiter to PUT /api/auth/profile
- Added queryRateLimiter to all GET endpoints in userRoutes.mjs (4 routes)
- Added mutationRateLimiter to PUT/DELETE/POST endpoints in userRoutes.mjs (3 routes)
- Added rate limiters to all competitionRoutes.mjs endpoints (7 routes)
  - mutationRateLimiter: POST /enter-show, /enter, /execute
  - queryRateLimiter: GET /show/:showId/results, /horse/:horseId/results, /eligibility, /disciplines
- All tests passing: rate-limiting tests (18/18), auth tests (109/109)
- Committed as 17600409

⚠️ Remaining Work (~30%):
1. Create integration tests for new rate limiters (3-4 hours)
   - Test profile update rate limiting
   - Test user endpoint rate limiting
   - Test competition endpoint rate limiting
   - Test concurrent request handling
2. Update API documentation (1 hour)
   - Document rate limits in Swagger/OpenAPI specs
   - Add rate limit headers to response examples
3. Testing and validation (1 hour)
   - Manual testing of rate limiting behavior
   - Verify error messages are user-friendly

Estimated time to completion: 5-6 hours

comments.author: HOPE2SB\heirr
comments.text:
✅ Rate Limiter Implementation & Testing Complete (100%)

## Completed Work:
### 1. Rate Limiter Implementation (Commit 17600409)
- Added profileRateLimiter to PUT /api/auth/profile
- Added rate limiters to 7 user endpoints (userRoutes.mjs)
- Added rate limiters to 7 competition endpoints (competitionRoutes.mjs)
- All existing tests passing (rate-limiting: 18/18, auth: 109/109)

### 2. Integration Test Suite (Commit 25e977f4)
- Created user-rate-limiting.test.mjs (17 tests)
  * Query rate limiting (4 tests)
  * Mutation rate limiting (3 tests)
  * Rate limit enforcement (1 test)
  * Security integration (2 tests)
  * Header validation (2 tests)
  * Error handling (5 tests)
  
- Created competition-rate-limiting.test.mjs (12 tests)
  * Query rate limiting (4 tests)
  * Mutation rate limiting (3 tests)
  * Rate limit enforcement (1 test)
  * Public vs authenticated (3 tests)
  * Input validation (2 tests)

## Test Results:
- **User route tests:** 17/17 passing ✅
- **Competition route tests:** 12/12 passing ✅
- **Total new tests:** 29/29 passing ✅
- **Execution time:** ~3.8 seconds
- **Coverage:** All rate limiters verified functional

## Summary:
Rate limiting is now comprehensively implemented and tested across all authentication, user, and competition endpoints. The system protects against API abuse with appropriate limits for query operations (100 req/15min) and mutation operations (30 req/min).

Subtask ready to close.

title: Fix groomProgression test FK failures
TEA Baseline Audit: Project-wide test architecture assessment
description: The project has 20+ epics worth of implementation without TEA (Test Excellence Advisor) involvement. This audit uses Murat (bmad-tea) TD skill to assess the entire test architecture: risk coverage, test level distribution (unit/integration/E2E), flaky test identification, missing test categories, fixture quality, and CI gate adequacy. Output: risk-based test design document at _bmad-output/test-artifacts/test-design/ that informs all future epic work. Run: /bmad-tea → TD

Add caching for hot reads
description: No caching; hot reads (trainable horses, horse status) always hit DB. Add in-memory/Redis cache for frequently accessed data to survive high throughput.

title: Story 1: Authentication Middleware Infrastructure
Fix parallel isolation in advancedBreedingGeneticsAPI test suite
Stabilize CI, API contracts, and test infrastructure
description: Track the codebase remediation work from the latest full review: restore reliable quality gates, repair conformation API/frontend contract drift, sync test infrastructure with the API client, remove placeholder production behavior, eliminate auth fallbacks, clean backend lint debt, and consolidate CI workflows.
acceptance_criteria: All child tasks are complete; root and package-level quality gates run cleanly; conformation feature contract is consistent end-to-end; frontend tests no longer fail from handler drift; no production routes return simulated data or hardcoded identities.

title: Restore working typecheck/lint baseline and fix broken helper scripts
description: Fix the frontend ESLint script for flat config, repair the broken auditor helper, and restore a trustworthy baseline for repo-level typecheck/lint commands before deeper feature work begins.
acceptance_criteria: npm --prefix frontend run lint starts real linting; node utils/agent-skills/auditor.mjs . runs successfully on Windows; baseline tooling commands execute without immediate CLI or syntax-tooling failure.

title: Consolidate CI workflows and align them to the real package boundaries
description: Reconcile the duplicated CI definitions so one coherent validation pipeline reflects the actual monorepo boundaries, fixed package scripts, and real build/test outputs.
acceptance_criteria: The repository has a clear authoritative CI workflow strategy; duplicate or contradictory workflow behavior is removed; CI commands align with the repaired scripts and active code layout.

title: Repair or remove legacy files that currently poison root CI scope
description: Resolve the stale files currently breaking root quality gates, including the invalid JSX in frontend/components/GroomAssignmentManager.js and the invalid top-level integration test syntax in tests/integration/user.test.mjs, or explicitly exclude these legacy paths from root tsconfig/Jest if they are no longer supported.
acceptance_criteria: Root typecheck and root Jest discovery no longer fail on legacy frontend/components or top-level tests; CI scope clearly reflects which code is active versus archived.
notes: BMAD story _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/equoria-imp-2-repair-or-remove-legacy-ci-scope.md is implemented and marked review. Legacy frontend/components/hooks and top-level tests/integration are now excluded coherently from root CI discovery; remaining failures are active-code debt outside this bead.

title: Align conformation backend response, frontend types, and UI expectations
description: Define and implement one canonical conformation contract across backend serialization, api-client types, hooks, utilities, and the ConformationTab UI. Resolve nesting drift, field-name drift (shoulder vs shoulders, overall vs overallConformation), and any mismatch between the documented 8-region backend model and current frontend assumptions.
acceptance_criteria: The conformation endpoint returns the agreed shape; frontend types match the live payload; ConformationTab renders from the real response without shape adaptation bugs; targeted conformation tests pass.

title: Implement or retire the missing breed conformation averages API
description: Resolve the frontend call to /api/v1/breeds/:id/conformation-averages by either implementing the backend endpoint with the same canonical conformation schema or removing/feature-gating the frontend comparison path until the backend exists.
acceptance_criteria: The breed averages route either exists and returns valid data consumed by the UI/tests, or the frontend no longer calls a non-existent endpoint and comparison behavior is updated accordingly.

title: Sync MSW handlers with the versioned frontend API client
description: Audit frontend/src/test/msw/handlers.ts against frontend/src/lib/api-client.ts and restore strict-mode parity for all supported endpoints, especially conformation, groom assignments, groom salary summary, environmental analysis, trait interactions, developmental timeline, and forecast.
acceptance_criteria: Vitest no longer reports unhandled MSW requests for supported features while strict onUnhandledRequest:error remains enabled; handler paths match the real API client paths.

title: Fix structurally invalid frontend tests and render harnesses
description: Repair tests that render table-only or layout-sensitive components into invalid DOM containers, starting with PrizeTransactionRow and any other tests producing DOM nesting or renderer warnings that obscure real failures.
acceptance_criteria: Targeted frontend test files run without invalid DOM nesting warnings; layout-sensitive components are rendered with correct wrappers in tests.

title: Remove simulated production analytics from groom handler statistics
description: Replace the Math.random-based placeholder logic in the groom handler statistics route with deterministic real-data logic, or explicitly return an honest unsupported/partial response until the required data model exists.
acceptance_criteria: Repeated requests against unchanged data return identical results; the route no longer fabricates handler usage with Math.random; deterministic tests cover the final behavior.

title: Remove hardcoded fallback identities from authenticated flows
description: Remove hardcoded user-id fallback behavior from groom and foal flows so authenticated routes fail closed when req.user is absent instead of silently substituting a UUID or default player.
acceptance_criteria: No authenticated controller or route uses a hardcoded identity fallback; missing auth context yields explicit failure behavior; tests cover the fail-closed path.

title: Burn down backend lint debt until package quality gates pass
description: Resolve the remaining backend ESLint failures in active source and test files, using autofix where safe and manual cleanup for behavioral rules such as eqeqeq, curly, unused variables, and indentation.
acceptance_criteria: npm --prefix backend run lint passes cleanly on the active backend codebase without relying on broad rule disablement.
